hypothesis: Replacing the template-based natural language prompt generation with a retrieval-augmented generation approach—where prompts for new target spectra are constructed by retrieving and adapting descriptions of structurally similar training examples—will reduce the validation generalization gap (currently ΔE ≈34) by at least 40% compared to the baseline CHROMA model, because retrieved analogous structures provide the decoder with implicit spectral neighborhood information that purely template-generated prompts cannot encode, thereby enabling better interpolation across unseen regions of the material-thickness combinatorial space.
